observations: Wordcount hunt: trimmed the gate paragraph to papers, seals, grain dust, and city noise instead of lingering on one extra sound beat.
Wordcount hunt: cut one redundant fear sentence from the guard read so boredom lands faster as the real danger.
Wordcount hunt: compressed the market inventory into working-city texture instead of a shopping list.
Wordcount hunt: tightened Corin's entrance by cutting repeated near-synonyms around how much he had collected.
Wordcount hunt: shortened Corin's route-report line without losing the bureaucratic stall pattern.
Wordcount hunt: reduced the exchange-office summary so Corin's usefulness stays earned but less narrated.
Wordcount hunt: cut the weather-joke overbuild in Corin's storeroom explanation and kept only the local-useful part.
Wordcount hunt: compressed Havel's entrance from full descriptive framing to nose, eyes, dust, and pressure.
Wordcount hunt: tightened Havel's suspect list so it reads as field pattern, not evidence recap.
Wordcount hunt: compressed the patrol sweep while keeping the key tells: no sweat, gloves on, paired movement, false paperwork, exit-checking.
Wordcount hunt: shortened the assay-room setup by cutting leak follow-up and one redundant explanatory clause.
Wordcount hunt: trimmed the closing route reflection while preserving the operational trust turn.
Hitlist hunt: line 81 no longer uses dossier language; 'Have you seen the mechanism?' became 'Have you seen how they're doing it?'
Hitlist hunt: line 223 killed 'body trigger' and replaced it with spoken-body language: 'What wakes it in the body?'
Hitlist hunt: line 243 killed 'alchemical trail preserved' and replaced it with the concrete 'trail on the boards intact.'
Hitlist hunt: line 225 killed 'packaging method plain' and moved Jaina back into field language about treatment and feeding it to a kingdom.
Hitlist hunt: line 263 killed 'city-side access' and flattened the capture instructions into speech Sylvanas would actually use out loud.
Hitlist hunt: removed repeated scaffolding around Corin's missing records so the joke stays nervous instead of AI-repetitive.
Hitlist hunt: removed one explanatory contrast from the split decision; the prose now lands directly on drag.
Hitlist hunt: kept the three remaining 'like' uses because they are character-grounded comparisons, not floating filler.
Dialogue hunt: Corin still sounds like a frightened junior mage talking too fast, not like a memo being read aloud.
Dialogue hunt: Jaina's assay explanation stays downshifted for Sylvanas and Havel while preserving magical precision.
Dialogue hunt: Sylvanas's tactical lines remain clipped, dry, and finished rather than sliding into briefing jargon.
Dialogue hunt: Havel's local-militia register stays plain and useful; no institutional ornament was added to his speech.
Dialogue hunt: the argument over proof versus capture now stays in living voice from both sides instead of note-language.
Meta-audit: re-read the densest paragraphs in the Havel briefing, assay reveal, and closing turn word by word after cuts.
Meta-audit: preserved the chapter's key payloads—the Andorhal machine, Corin's competence, Havel's yard read, the treatment reveal, and Sylvanas accepting dependence on Jaina's unseen work.

changes.before: The street beyond the gate opened into a market broad enough for three wagon lines and still crowded at the edges with bread, onions, lamp oil, tack, cured meat...
changes.after: Beyond the gate ran a market broad enough for three wagon lines, its edges packed with the ordinary things a working city bought without thought.

changes.before: The one at the back looked as though he had forgotten to sleep for a month.
changes.after: The one at the back looked half dead from lack of sleep.

changes.before: I have almost everything. Some of it went missing, but I copied most of what went missing before it missed itself.
changes.after: I have almost everything. Some went missing, but I copied most of it before it missed itself.
Have you seen how they're doing it?

changes.before: They found Havel in the granary district itself, because that was where a man with his face belonged.
changes.after: They found Havel in the granary district itself.

changes.before: Keeping both halves together any longer would not be caution. It would be drag.
changes.after: Keeping both halves together any longer would be drag.
What wakes it in the body?

changes.before: Andorhal gives us the packaging method plain. Whoever runs this place isn't poisoning grain by guesswork. They're dosing a kingdom.
changes.after: Andorhal shows us the treatment plain. Whoever runs this place isn't poisoning grain by guesswork. They're feeding it to a kingdom.

changes.before: I still want the alchemical trail preserved.
changes.after: I still want the trail on the boards intact.

changes.before: Corin keeps the city-side access quiet and closes the mage doors behind us. Jaina reads whatever the prisoner gives us against what she found upstairs.
changes.after: Corin keeps the city doors quiet and closes the mage rooms behind us. Jaina reads the prisoner against what she found upstairs.

changes.before: By the time the plan held, the city outside had shifted into its night shape: wagon noise gone, tavern noise up, a different economy moving through the streets.
changes.after: By the time the plan held, the city outside had shifted into its night shape: wagon noise gone, tavern noise up.

changes.before: She disliked naming what had changed. The names came out soft, and the thing itself was not.
changes.after: She disliked naming what had changed. It made the thing sound softer than it was.

flags: Kept three 'like' comparisons; final count stays at the chapter cap and each is tied to POV judgment rather than decorative simile fog.
Kept one literal 'something' usage in 'something she could use fast' because it is object-indeterminate, not vague emotional filler.
Preserved the closing trust line unchanged because it is the chapter's load-bearing emotional turn and already stays in Sylvanas's operational frame.
